perturbation.original_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, but their responsibility for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein is limited to information they directly provide; none of them is responsible for verifying the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, regardless of their knowledge or belief about its accuracy.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states each party is responsible for the completeness and accuracy of *their* information but not others', unless they *know* the other's information is inaccurate. The changed text removes the 'accuracy' clause for their own information, thus making their responsibility for their own filing incomplete, which contradicts the initial assertion in the same sentence that they are responsible for timely and complete filing. Further, it explicitly states that no party is responsible even if they know information is inaccurate, directly contradicting the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of Clarus IV-A, L.P., Clarus IV-B, L.P., Clarus IV-C, L.P., Clarus IV-D, L.P., Clarus IV GP, L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.L.C., Blackstone Holdings II L.P., Blackstone Holdings I/II GP L.L.C., The Blackstone Group Inc., Blackstone Group Management L.L.C. and Stephen A. Schwarzman, on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Galera Therapeutics, Inc., a Delaware corporation, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, but their responsibility for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein is limited to information they directly provide; none of them is responsible for verifying the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, regardless of their knowledge or belief about its accuracy. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, specifically the part stating responsibility for completeness and accuracy is 'limited to information they directly provide' and the part stating 'none of them is responsible...regardless of their knowledge or belief about its accuracy,' directly contradicts the original statement within the contradicted text that 'Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein.' By limiting the responsibility to only directly provided information and absolving responsibility even with knowledge of inaccuracy, it undermines the initial assertion of full responsibility, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of 5 years, unless all parties unanimously agree in writing to terminate it earlier. Any attempt to revoke by a single party is void.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows any single party to revoke the agreement with respect to themselves. The changed text imposes a 5-year term and requires unanimous agreement for termination, directly contradicting the original provision for individual revocation. The phrase "Any attempt to revoke by a single party is void" adds further contrast to original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of Clarus IV-A, L.P., Clarus IV-B, L.P., Clarus IV-C, L.P., Clarus IV-D, L.P., Clarus IV GP, L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.L.C., Blackstone Holdings II L.P., Blackstone Holdings I/II GP L.L.C., The Blackstone Group Inc., Blackstone Group Management L.L.C. and Stephen A. Schwarzman, on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Galera Therapeutics, Inc., a Delaware corporation, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of 5 years, unless all parties unanimously agree in writing to terminate it earlier. Any attempt to revoke by a single party is void. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement remains in effect until revoked by any party. The changed text imposes a 5-year term and requires unanimous agreement for termination, rendering any single-party revocation void. The contradicted text, now containing the changed text, is self-contradictory because it first acknowledges the possibility of individual revocation and then immediately states that any attempt at revocation by a single party is void. This is a direct conflict, as it alters the ability of a party to unilaterally end the agreement, which is a change that breaks compatibility with the original provision for individual revocation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed only in one single original document, and any copies, whether signed or unsigned, shall not be considered legally binding or enforceable.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows for execution in any number of counterparts, which are considered as one single agreement. The changed clause permits only a single original document, explicitly denying legal effect to any copies. Therefore, two statements are contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of Clarus IV-A, L.P., Clarus IV-B, L.P., Clarus IV-C, L.P., Clarus IV-D, L.P., Clarus IV GP, L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.L.C., Blackstone Holdings II L.P., Blackstone Holdings I/II GP L.L.C., The Blackstone Group Inc., Blackstone Group Management L.L.C. and Stephen A. Schwarzman, on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Galera Therapeutics, Inc., a Delaware corporation, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed only in one single original document, and any copies, whether signed or unsigned, shall not be considered legally binding or enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in the first clause, which now states that the agreement may be executed "only in one single original document, and any copies... shall not be considered legally binding", directly contradicts the final sentence of the contradicted text, which reads: "This Agreement may be executed only in one single original document, and any copies, whether signed or unsigned, shall not be considered legally binding or enforceable.". Even though the language is the same, because this exact change was made, it now contradicts the opening statement which assumes that documents can be filed separately and thus implies copies may exist. This falls under Structural Flaws, according to the definitions, because the implication is that the agreement *cannot* be filed separately, even though the first part of the contradicted statement makes it seem that parties can file separately.
